content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Ezzat and Grid". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/contest/1557/problem/D
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, which is the number of rows and columns in the grid. Then n lines follow, each containing n integers.
content.outputFormat: The output should be a single integer, which is the minimum number of operations required to make all elements in the grid equal.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a breadth-first search (BFS) or depth-first search (DFS) algorithm to traverse the grid and find the minimum number of operations required to make all elements equal.

code.Python3:
n = int(input())
grid = [list(map(int, input().split())) for _ in range(n)]
# implement BFS or DFS algorithm here
